occupation: Chief Executive Officer at Liquid Marketing, Inc. | LMI Advisory Group
headline: Founder / CEO of Liquid Marketing Inc. (LMI Advisory Group)  |   CEO of SmartBomb Media Group |
summary:
In addition to developing in-store experience platforms and mobile marketing initiatives, Sheldon Silverman has spent the last 39 years as an advocate of integrated marketing strategies that combine brand awareness with actionable platforms. He has not only founded and led successful marketing firms and agencies but also has helped launch technology companies that extend the channels with which consumers and brands communicate. Sheldon has worked with companies such as Uber, Discover, Jenny Craig, SallieMae, QVC, Netflix, Sony Pictures, JiffyLube, Sears, ShopNBC, Citi, Guthy Renker, Farmers Insurance, T-Mobile, Eddie Bauer and others to enhance brand equity while creating integrated marketing & experiential campaigns.

Some of Sheldon's awards include the 2014 United Nations Leadership Award for Driving Social Change Through Media, the Global SpeechTek Award for Best Voice Application & multiple CADM Tempo Awards.

Sheldon is the Founder & Chairman of Liquid Marketing / LMI Advisory Group. As a consultant / advisor in the OOH Media space, Sheldon has worked with a wide range of companies that are leaders in the industry, including Broadsign, Absen, ARA, Grupo Vallas, SOMO, Plan B, Dentsu, INGKA, IKEA, All Unite, PlayNetwork, iCoMedios, VSBLTY, LG/MRI, SmartPoint, OutFront, ClearChannel, Praevar and many others. Sheldon has also worked closely with the investment community advising the likes of Metropolitan Partners, Clay Path Partners, Celeres Investments, Alpine Ventures and Maveron.

Sheldon was on the Inaugural Leadership Committee for the UN Media For Social Impact Summit as well as participating on a White House advisory council on Multicultural Outreach during the Obama Presidency. Sheldon is on the Board of Trustees of the Automotive Aftermarket Foundation & was a longstanding member of the Board of Directors for the Digital Signage Federation. As part of his work with the DSF, Sheldon Silverman created the DSF Global DOOH Council, launched in 2017. The aim of the council is to foster educating brands and agencies on the power of DOOH & Place Based Media, create standards for the industry and drive the adoption of integrated API’s that will allow media agencies and brands to simplify the process of buying DOOH and its integration into the other communication touchpoints that’s brands utilize. In 2025, Sheldon helped form and was named the Chairman of the OAAA Commerce / Retail Media Working Group.

Sheldon is a frequent speaker & author in North America, South America, UAE & Europe

experiences.company: Liquid Marketing, Inc. | LMI Advisory Group
experiences.title: Chief Executive Officer
experiences.description:
LMI Advisory Group is a bespoke Global Out-of-Home / DOOH / Urban Media & Retail Media advisory & consulting firm. Sheldon Silverman, Founder and CEO, has worked with many of the leading companies in the Urban Media & Retail Media landscape, from North America to Europe and Asia, to assist them with their long-term growth plans as well as OOH Digitization, JV / Acquisitions and high-level industry partnerships.  Sheldon is the Chair of the OAAA Commerce / Retail Media working group. Sheldon was also on the Board of the Digital Signage Federation for 8 years and was the Co-Chair of the DSF Global Digital Out-of-Home Council.  Sheldon is the only non-automotive industry executive on the Automotive Aftermarket Charitable Foundation.

Liquid Marketing got its start over 34 years ago as a full-service brand and direct marketing agency that created award winning integrated campaigns as well as developing in-store Digital Out-of-Home (DOOH) advertising spots and campaigns for in-store retail digital networks. The firm then evolved to be a media, technology and experiential consultancy. LMI has a long-standing reputation for also building leading edge interactive systems including mobile apps and loyalty marketing platforms integrating POS systems with robust analytic engines.

experiences.title: Global Advisor to Hypervsn
experiences.description: HYPERVSN is the leading 3D holographic technology, backed by years of innovation, creating powerful and immersive holographic experiences that captivate audiences.  Leading global brands and agencies partner with us to to deliver exceptional experiences that stop people in their tracks, in retail, airports, malls, train stations, public spaces, stadiums and now on the street with our Hypervsn Holo-Trucks. Capture attention with Hypervsn and drive new levels of earned media and engagement.

experiences.title: CEO
experiences.description:
SmartBomb Media Group is fast becoming a leader in the Out-of-Home Digital Media and Mobile Marketing space.  The core business is developing, deploying and managing in-store digital media platforms that connect with the mobile consumer.  Included in these platforms is the ability to close the loop by initiating SMS Messaging, QR, NFC and other touch-point connections with the viewer.

iCASHtv is the largest digital in-store television network focused on Urban Multicultural Consumers in very high traffic Check Cashing locations in major DMA's.  iCASHtv delivers a mix of lifestyle content and brand/product advertising to reach this highly valuable consumer in their "last mile to purchase decision".  As the largest US DOOH Network focused on the Check Cashing consumer, with a core demographic of Hispanic viewers, iCASHtv is a partner of Telemundo as well and drives actionable advertising impressions to the Latino consumer.

experiences.title: Head of Global Media Sponsorships & Advertising | Partner
experiences.description: We're a Los Angeles, CA based Global Media Collective

experiences.title: Chairman
experiences.description:
The Digital Signage Federation Global Digital Out-of-Home (DOOH) Council has a worldwide purview specifically to support the needs of companies involved in the intersection between out-of-home advertising and the digital signage industry.  The primary objective of the DSF Global Digital Out-of-Home Council is to provide a resource for DSF members to build their capacity to support out-of-home advertising companies, agencies, and brands. Much of this capacity will be developed through conformity of analytics, ad buying, and technology deployment. 
My Co-Chairs are Michael Cooper, Global President & CEO of Rapport Worldwide & Kym Frank, President of Geopath.

http://www.digitalsignagefederation.org/about/global-digital-out-of-home-dooh-council/#.Wg4pOq3GwUE

experiences.title: Co-Chair - Network Operators Subcommittee
experiences.description: The Network Operators Subcommittee of the DSF is focused on furthering the business objectives,  providing a unified voice and fostering the ecosystem that digital signage network operators can thrive in.

experiences.title: Member of the Board of Directors
experiences.description: DSF's Mission is to support and promote the common business interests of the world-wide digital signage, interactive technologies and the digital out-of-home network industries.

experiences.title: Board of Trustees Member
experiences.description:
Sheldon helps lead the marketing and public relations initiatives for the Aftermarket Foundation and is the only non-aftermarket industry member of the board.

Founded in 1959, the Aftermarket Foundation’s mission for over half a century, is to assist individuals and families from the automotive aftermarket industry  who have fallen on extreme hard times because of death, catastrophic illness or other financial hardship.

The Foundation provides financial assistance but, in many cases, is able to channel claimants to other established, conventional sources of help and support, including consultations and counselling, where applicable.

The Foundation is organized as a charitable organization, registered under IRS 501(c)(3), and contributions to the Foundation are tax deductible.  The Foundation is run by leading industry executive volunteers and one, hard-working and dedicated manager.  Thus far, we have been able tocontain all overheads to within 10%, of revenue, leaving 90% available for actual assistance.

www.aftermarketfoundation.org

experiences.title: Partner
experiences.description:
At OOH Capital, we help a wide range of clients, from cities and municipalities to brands, advertising agencies, media networks, adtech companies and private equity / investment banks realize  value from the limitless potential of OOH media.

We are comprehensive in capability, international in reach, and extensive in expertise. An enviable blend of skills. From assessment and evaluation, through to strategy, deployment and delivery, OOH Capital is the complete partner.  Our partners have been leaders in the OOH / Media Industry on almost every continent and bring that combined knowledge to our clients.

OOH Capital. The Urban Media Experts.

WE HELP CITIES GET SMARTER + COMMUNITIES TO THRIVE
Creating smarter cities and better places

WE NURTURE BUSINESS TRANSFORMATION
Advising on investments, digitization and opportunites

WE HELP COMPANIES BUILD FOR THE FUTURE + EMBRACE COLLABORATION
Ensuring best-in-class procurement processes

WE CULTIVATE WINNING BUSINESS STRATEGIES + FOSTER COMMERCIALIZATION
Monetizing assets for growth

WE MAKE SENSE OF CHANGING BUSINESS DYNAMICS + EXPAND INSIGHTS
Planning, activating, and interpreting research

WE NAVIGATE THE UNCERTAINTY OF DIGITAL TRANSFORMATION + TECHNOLOGY
Developing and optimizing tech strategies

experiences.title: Partner | CMO | CSO
experiences.description: Barology® Bars are the Best Tasting, Gluten-Free, Low-Sugar, High-Fiber, High-Protein Nutrition Bars Created for the Differing & Specific Needs of Men and Women.  We spent years working with the best US based food scientists and flavor houses to design nutrition bars that not only are great for you, but that truly taste great. Barology is bringing The Science of Functional Nutrition® to the Protein Bar market.

experiences.title: Managing Director
experiences.description: Sheldon was brought in to DMS from an executive search by the San Francisco Venture Fund that was an owner of DMS.  As the Managing Director, Sheldon headed up the integrated strategy and client development at the agency in order to gain creative agency contracts which also lead to major print production business.  DMS is a 25+-year-old integrated DM Agency and Production shop focused on high touch, boutique level service and turnkey solutions.  With an average client tenure of over 7 years, DMS catered to demanding clients such as Citi, SallieMae, BlueCross/BlueShield, Comcast, BlueSky Auto Finance and many others.  DMS focused on production, mailing over 15 million pieces per month at a SAS70 II certified production facility.  Sheldon's role was to enhance the agency and creative services.

experiences.title: SVP, Strategy Development
experiences.description:
•	As the SVP of Strategy Development, Sheldon lead the creation of the Protocol Virtual Agent Voice Application Integrated Contact Center strategy and platform 
•	Trained entire sales force in the Virtual Agent strategy and developed collateral presentation material for the business development group.  Involved in strategy consultation for most major Protocol Virtual Agent opportunities and co-lead the partner integration negotiations  
•	Lead the Virtual Agent and DM integration Strategy for the Board of Directors of Protocol
•	In the initial few months at Protocol, closed the first United Kingdom deal that resulted in 22 day revenue in excess of $440,000.  In January of 2003, closed a new business deal for, Intuit, one of the top five Protocol clients, which resulted in over $750,000 of revenue in four months. executive in charge on two of the largest clients in the call center division with combined ’03 revenue of over $9 million
•	Co-lead executive on the integrated platform sale to a major telecom which was the largest client in the call center division in ’04 - ongoing yearly revenue for in-bound customer care projected $6 million, outbound $2 million, loyalty CRM $1 million and Direct Marketing Agency revenue of $1 million 
•                In January ‘o4 promoted to the Corporate Agency Services Division and immediately  acquired agency business contracts with GE Financial and First Midwest
•	In 2004, I brought in new integrated DM agency client contracts (some as Agency of Record) with SallieMae, Jenny Craig, Discover and others which were projected to generate over $15 million in ’05 revenue.  In January of 2005, received an increased budget by $1 million from a West Coast insurance client for roll-out of a Print on Demand/Campaign Web Portal platform we developed for insurance agents across the US.

experiences.title: EVP
experiences.description:
•	Created the technology & sales consulting practice in February of 2001 and headed up the engagement with our first client, BridgeDialog, as a founding member
•	Within the first three months, secured business that resulted in over $450,000 per year in consulting retainer fees alone
•	Co-created, with a major client, an integrated database/POS marketing system for the automotive industry. Sold the platform service as an ASP to one of the largest Automotive Service Center companies in North America for a 2002 regional launch and then a national roll-out. First year revenue expected to be over $6 million to my consulting client for this contract

experiences.title: SVP - Consultant
experiences.description:
•	Brought on as a consultant to the founders and given an equity stake and SVP title. Co-wrote the business plan for BridgeDialog that was used to architect the company and raise capital
•	Co-inventor of the Voice Cart & Voice Wallet (Patent Pending) technology – voice activated electronic shopping cart and wallet
•	Presented to most of the initial venture funds and angel investors in order to obtain funding commitments
•	Closed BridgeDialog’s main enterprise platform clients, ShopNBC and QVC, and created the QVC QAlert inbound/outbound platform that received some of the highest satisfaction ratings (Q Ratings) of any customer contact program QVC had initiated.  QVC acted as an ongoing customer and capital raising reference for BridgeDialog
•	Obtained a commitment from Sears for an enterprise level voice platform initiative within Sears Commercial/Industrial sales as a test for a complete 62 call center enterprise platform for all of Sears.  Sears acted as an ongoing customer and capital raising reference for BridgeDialog
•	Lead the sales integration for BridgeDialog in relation to the partnership with Protocol and trained both the East and West Coast CRM sales groups in the Voice Driven Virtual Agent platform.

experiences.title: Vice President
experiences.description:
•	Helped launch the company as a consultant at the beginning of 1998
•	As the consultant, wrote the white paper that become the sales/marketing blueprint for the 1-800-ShopTalk consumer voice portal, the first interactive voice portal ever
•	Joined the company as the VP after the consulting period ended and worked closely with Dan Levitan and the Board of Directors on strategic client development 
•	Championed and architected the switch to an enterprise voice platform business in 2000  
•	Closed over 90% of all of the ShopTalk business between 1998 and February 2001, including banner clients QVC, Pizza Hut, Blockbuster, Jiffy Lube, EarthLink, Spiegel and Eddie Bauer
•	The Oil Change Reminder Platform I created and sold to Jiffy Lube won the Speech Technology Magazine Editors Choice Award for Most Innovative Speech Solution in 2001 
•	Presenter at the 2000 Maveron Customer Acquisition Conference.  Maveron is the largest Pacific Northwest VC Fund and is managed by Howard Schultz, CEO of Starbucks, and Dan Levitan

accomplishment_projects.title: Article on Ara and GPO Vallas Joint Venture Rollout of SOMA, NYC's largest Car Top Digital Ad Network
accomplishment_projects.description: GPO Vallas, a leading Latin American “out-of-home” (OOH) advertising company announces the U.S. launch of SOMO, a new business line built by partnering with Ara Labs, for the operation of 4,000 screens in 2,000 digital car top advertising displays in NYC, which generate over 3 billion monthly impressions.  SHELDON SILVERMAN was a lead adviser on this venture.

accomplishment_projects.title: Retail Media Article from Media 4 Growth
accomplishment_projects.description: Media 4 Growth Article on Sheldon Silverman's Retail Media presentation from the 2024 World Out of Home Conference in Hong Kong.  The article is titled "Building OOH Partnerships with Retail Media Networks"

accomplishment_projects.title: How Connecting Independent OOH Operators is Accelerating Revenues in OOH Today
accomplishment_projects.description: At this year’s Digital Sign Expo in Las Vegas, one of the top Tuesday sessions of the DOOH Strategy Summit, discussed the many different networks, platforms, technologies and standards which comprises the Digital Out of Home ecosystem.  The message from SHELDON SILVERMAN was clear; embrace open architecture software design for OOH.

accomplishment_projects.title: "Digital Out-of-Home Drivers and Headwinds" by SHELDON SILVERMAN in Sound and Communications Magazine
accomplishment_projects.description: It was the best of times; it was the worst of times. The year gone by was a fantastic one for digital out-of-home (DOOH), and, at the same time, a problematic one by the end of the year. The numbers tell a great story, with increases in media spend, dedicated DOOH dollars and further integration within the mobile marketing ecosystem.

accomplishment_projects.title: Podcast with David Haynes Sixteen Nine about GDPR and its affects on the OOH Industry
accomplishment_projects.description: If you are in digital signage or digital out of home, in particular, you should care privacy policies. GDPR is a problem for a lot of digital media companies, but an opportunity for those that use anonymous data, like a lot of place-based media operators. Sheldon Silverman, the CEO of Chicago-based SmartBomb Media Group, has his head around all this. So I asked Silverman, who also heads the Digital Signage Federation's Global Digital OOH Council, if we could do a podcast to fill the rest of us in. Get a pen and paper. This is worth taking notes ...

accomplishment_projects.title: SmartBomb Media Group Partnership with BlueBite Announced in Times Square DOOH
accomplishment_projects.description: BlueBite's partnership with SmartBomb Media Group is expanding the use of NFC M-Tags and connecting consumers in Retail Media Networks.

accomplishment_projects.title: Digital Signage Pulse Article “Programmatic and Digital Out-of-Home – When Will DOOH Get its Share?”  by Sheldon Silverman
accomplishment_projects.description: Let’s face it – for the last three years you couldn’t miss an article, conference session or strategy white paper appearing almost every month on the promise of programmatic buying in the digital out-of-home (DOOH) advertising. In that same time period, while our industry has made strides forward, the reality has not yet lived up to the promise.  But, that may be about to change…

accomplishment_projects.title: DSF Global Digital Out-of-Home Council Launch
accomplishment_projects.description: The Digital Signage Federation announced the first global digital out-of-home-focused organization at Digital Signage Expo 2017 in Las Vegas, Nevada.

accomplishment_projects.title: rAVe Article “Feeding the Beast: Content Strategies for Digital Signage” by Sheldon Silverman
accomplishment_projects.description: With the explosive growth of digital signage in all business sectors, many have embraced the mantra of Kevin Costner’s character in Field of Dreams — “If you build it, they will come.” The bigger question in this for digital signage network owners and operators is, “Will they watch?” Content for digital signage is an often overlooked, yet key cornerstone of the success of any platform

accomplishment_projects.title: Currents Magazine article "Financial Service Centers Driving Social Change Through Media" INDUSTRY LEADING IN MULTICULTURAL COMMUNITY OUTREACH | BY SHELDON SILVERMAN
accomplishment_projects.description: With all of the dissonance between regulators, legislators and the Financial Service Centers (FSCs) vertical, there are bright spots showing how responsible our industry is in regard to fostering the well-being of our community of customers. For years, our industry has participated in Public Service campaigns aimed at our multicultural consumers, fostering attention to health and wellness, education and fiscal responsibility.

accomplishment_projects.title: iCASHtv/SmartBomb Media Group CEO given the Leadership Award for Driving Social Change Through Media at the United Nations Media for Social Impact Summit in 2014
accomplishment_projects.description: “iCASHtv was picked for the Drink Up campaign because Nielsen had identified the Hispanic and African American demographics as key targets for this campaign, and iCASHtv delivers these audience segments in high concentration. The type of venue and the long dwell time within the venue, combined with the key audience delivery, made iCASHtv a perfect partner for our campaign. This was proven true in Nielsen’s data,” according to Daniel Wilkins, the Chair of Media Partnerships at the PVBLIC Foundation and Founder of Agency 672. Paul Lindstrom, SVP Custom Research at Nielsen, states “The findings of the Drink Up Campaign study by Nielsen exhibit data for consumer engagement that is very strong for Digital Place-Based media as a whole. In regard to the all-important Consumer Intent question ‘I plan to drink more water in the future,’ iCASHtv was one of the highest scoring networks in the study. Seeing a highly targeted multicultural network like iCASHtv scoring so positively bodes well for networks that target their consumer focus appropriately.” CASHtv reached this very important multicultural consumer segment on such a key health-related issue, and not only did the network score as one of the highest in “indexed lift,” but because of its participation in this campaign the iCASHtv/SmartBomb Media Group CEO won the Leadership Award for Driving Social Change Through Media at the United Nations Media for Social Impact Summit.

accomplishment_projects.title: Franchise Times Article “Signs of the Times - More Brands Turn to Digital Displays to Reach Customers"
accomplishment_projects.description: Sheldon Silverman, CEO of SmartBomb Media in Chicago, has been in the digital signage game for three years as a vendor, developing a network of digital signs in check-cashing locations throughout the country that generate revenue through advertising. The young firm is a growth vehicle separate from his older, more established traditional advertising agency.

accomplishment_projects.title: The store is still the star: Takeaways from the DSA symposium - The Digital Screenmedia Association's recent event highlighted the innovation of Retail Media and in-store technology.
accomplishment_projects.description: Rich Ventura, director of sales for NEC, moderated a panel called "Form, Fit and Function," featuring representatives from United Airlines, SmartBomb Media Group (a DOOH network) and VS Networks (providers of John Deere's in-store kiosks and digital signage). While the panelists addressed topics such as the right screen for the job, nearly 60 digital signage examples were shown in a looping presentation on the screen.

accomplishment_projects.title: Checklist Magazine "Approach is Critical in Branding, Marketing" by Sheldon Silverman
accomplishment_projects.description: That ancient Chinese curse, “May you live in interesting times,” may have been directed squarely at the check cashing industry of today. With governmental oversight and rules becoming ever more prohibitive and encroachment from traditional big box players, such as Wal-Mart, it is harder than ever for the check casher of today to not only flourish, but also to compete effectively.

accomplishment_projects.title: Checklist Magazine Article on Multicultural Marketing "Don’t Let Yourself be Lost in Translation” by Sheldon Silverman
accomplishment_projects.description: Are you feeling like a character from the movie “Lost in Translation”? Does it feel as if you no longer have a handle on who your customers are, or how to choose which products and services best fit their needs while still addressing your business needs?  With the plethora of choices your customer has in this evolved industry, there is a real need to diversify and target your products appropriately and deliver offerings that your customers will embrace.

accomplishment_projects.description: Television and DOOH Brand and DRTV Spots on Vimeo

recommendations:
Tommi Marsans

I have worked with Sheldon on several projects and above all that I can say is that he 'gets it'. He can quickly understand new and challenging business models and can identify needs and opportunities almost instantly. With his wide network he is able to pull in expertise that others are not able to and this brings fresh perspective and innovation to his recommendations. He is a pleasure to work with.

Ira Krell

Over the years, I have worked with Sheldon on multiple projects.  I can safely say, few people understand the check cashing/retail financial services industry the way he does.   This coupled with his creativity and expertise with digital signage allow him to deliver marketing campaigns with high ROI.   Just as important, Sheldon's strategic approach focuses on measurable results so you can quickly determine a program's success.
